fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 12-year-old male brought by mother for mild anxiety about starting middle school.
fields.Subjective: Patient is a 12-year-old male presenting with nervousness about starting middle school next month. He reports worrying about finding classes, having different teachers, and managing locker combination. He denies any thoughts of self-harm or suicidal ideation. Patient states most of his friends will be at same school which provides reassurance. He attended school orientation last week and felt better after seeing the building. Sleep and appetite normal. Looking forward to joining robotics club. Mother brought him for evaluation to ensure he has resources if needed but notes patient has successfully transitioned through changes before. Patient appears to have age-appropriate concerns that are not interfering with function. Engaged in summer activities including camp. (Social Worker Denise Phillips, LCSW)
fields.Diagnostic_Impression: Normal anticipatory anxiety regarding school transition, mild.
fields.Psychosocial_Context: Lives with both parents and younger sister; experiencing age-appropriate concerns about middle school transition; attended orientation; friends will attend same school; history of successful transitions; supportive family.
Assessment_and_Plan: Age-appropriate anticipatory anxiety about developmental milestone; no pathology.
Protective factors: friends at same school, attended orientation, history of successful transitions, engaged in extracurriculars, supportive family, no suicidal ideation.
Reassurance provided that concerns are normal and adaptive.
Encouraged continued preparation activities (practicing locker, reviewing schedule).
Normalize adjustment period for first few weeks of school.
Plan for early arrival on first day to ease transition.
No intervention needed; expect successful adjustment.
Resources provided if concerns emerge after school starts.
fields.Author: Denise Phillips, LCSW (Social Worker)
